ac:rich-text-body.p: Observatory Data Products (ODPs) are standard pipeline outputs from SKA. Advanced Data Products (ADPs) are community-derived science products built from ODPs.

p: The SKA produces two categories of data products that feed scientific discovery: Observatory Data Products (ODPs) produced by the SKA data processing pipeline, and Advanced Data Products (ADPs) developed by the research community or the observatory.

Observatory Data Products are standardised, calibrated outputs of the SKA Science Data Processor (SDP). They represent the primary data products guaranteed to be produced and archived for every science observation.

ul.li: Continuum Images: Integrated intensity maps across broad frequency ranges, suitable for source detection, morphology studies, and wide-field surveys
Spectral-Line Cubes: Three-dimensional data with two spatial dimensions and frequency dimension, enabling velocity-resolved science
Visibility Data: Raw calibrated interferometric visibilities, enabling independent re-analysis and alternative processing approaches
Pulsar Timing Products: High-precision arrival time measurements, timing residuals, and pulse profiles from pulsar observations
Detected Filterbank Data: Time-frequency data for transient and pulsar searches
Flowthrough Archive Data: Subset of calibrated visibilities archived continuously for serendipitous discovery and reprocessing
Standardised: Produced using consistent pipeline configurations and algorithms
Quality Assured: Subject to automated quality checks and validation
Reproducible: Includes full processing metadata for reproducibility and validation
Archived: Permanently stored in the SKA Archive with indexed metadata
Discoverable: Findable via the SKA Archive portal with comprehensive search and filtering

p: Advanced Data Products are higher-level science products derived from ODPs. ADPs are produced by the research community, international collaborations, or the SKA observatory itself, and represent value-added analysis of ODP data.

ul.li: Source Catalogues: Lists of detected sources with properties (positions, fluxes, classifications) extracted from continuum images
Spectral-Line Catalogues: Detections and measurements from spectral-line cubes (e.g., HI surveys, galaxy redshifts)
Mosaics: Combination of multiple observations into larger sky maps
Stacked Products: Co-addition of multiple observations or sources for enhanced sensitivity
Derived Properties: Physical quantities computed from ODP measurements (e.g., stellar masses, star formation rates)
Machine Learning Models: Trained classifiers, regression models, or anomaly detection outputs
Community-generated: Produced by research teams using public ODP data and their own analysis tools
Collaboration products: Generated by large survey collaborations or working groups
Observatory-curated: High-value ADPs validated and endorsed by the SKA observatory for broad community use

p: Community-generated ADPs can be submitted to the SKA Archive for curation and long-term preservation, making them discoverable alongside ODPs.
ODPs and ADPs form a data processing continuum:

tr.td: ODP ← Raw calibrated observation product from SDP pipeline
ADP ← Science-ready higher-level product (catalogues, mosaics, etc.)

p: This design allows the SKA to:

ul.li: Provide robust, standardised data products to all users
Enable diverse science analyses through the ADP ecosystem
Avoid reproducing pipeline computations unnecessarily
Preserve the intellectual contributions of research teams

p: All ODPs undergo automated quality checks including:

ul.li: Beam and noise characterisation
Astrometric and photometric calibration validation
Radio frequency interference (RFI) flagging quality
Pipeline execution logs and error checking

p: Quality metrics are recorded in the ODP metadata for user assessment.
Community ADPs carry detailed provenance information documenting:

ul.li: Source ODP identifiers and versions
Analysis software, algorithms, and parameters
Validation and testing procedures
Known limitations and caveats

p: This transparency enables users to assess the reliability and applicability of ADPs for their science.
h2: Integration with the SKA Science Archive
p: Both ODPs and ADPs are discoverable and accessible through the centralised SKA Science Archive:

ul.li: Unified search: Find ODPs and ADPs with common query interfaces
Metadata indexing: Full-text and structured searches across all product attributes
Download and streaming: Access raw files or stream subsets on demand
Computational access: Run analysis directly on archive-hosted data via cloud services

ac:rich-text-body.p: If you are developing Advanced Data Products, see the ADP submission guidelines in the Archive documentation to register your products and make them discoverable to the global community.
